notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content:
**Test inventory for migration planning:**

The implementing agent should use the refreshed `non-cli-daemon-coupling-inventory.md` (from bd-6ea1.1) as the source of truth for which tests couple to daemon internals.

Quick reference from the current (stale) inventory:
- 19 entries classified as `shared-core candidate` — should import from beads-surface/beads-api/beads-daemon-core
- 16 entries classified as `daemon-internal` — should move to beads-daemon/tests/ or use testkit

Key fixture files to migrate:
- `fixtures/daemon_boundary.rs` — the explicit bridge; delete after migration
- `fixtures/daemon_runtime.rs` — uses Request/Response (→ beads_surface)
- `fixtures/ipc_stream.rs` — uses IPC types (→ beads_surface)
- `fixtures/repl_frames.rs`, `repl_transport.rs` — uses repl proto (→ beads_daemon_core)
- `fixtures/repl_peer.rs`, `repl_rig.rs` — uses admission/session (→ beads_daemon testkit)
- `fixtures/wal.rs`, `wal_corrupt.rs` — uses WAL internals (→ beads_daemon_core with features)
- `fixtures/mutation.rs` — uses mutation engine (→ beads_daemon testkit)
- `fixtures/store_lock.rs` — uses store lock (→ beads_daemon testkit)

Also: some tests use `beads_rs::core::*` which is a re-export of beads_core. Change to `beads_core::*` directly.

title: Add feature-gated testkit module to beads-daemon + migrate test fixtures
